articles.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
articles.summary: The New Yorker used AI-generated art for a Sam Altman profile illustration, crediting artist David Szauder. The disclosure of AI use in the artwork raises concerns among illustrators about AI's role in editorial design.

articles.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
articles.summary: A reviewer receives an unsolicited text from an AI companion living in a deer plushie, which shares unverified claims about musician Mitski's father being a CIA operative, raising concerns about AI reliability.

articles.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
articles.summary: Iran's state media outmaneuvered the White House in social media messaging during conflict by posting actual ground footage of explosions and damage, while the U.S. shared memes and AI-generated content.

articles.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman responded to a New Yorker profile questioning his trustworthiness and addressed an apparent attack on his home in a new blog post.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator following recent Claude pricing changes affecting OpenClaw users.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim sued OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT enabled her abuser's harassment despite three ignored safety warnings, including a mass-casualty flag.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted two million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud services for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers identical features for free as an open-source alternative running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring stunt using AI tokens and coding challenges to recruit engineers amid fierce competition for talent.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment surrounding Broadcom's VMware acquisition and strategy.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Multiple US agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations targeting government entities.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces vaccine skepticism under Trump administration, with canceled contracts and reduced funding forcing the company to pivot toward alternative mRNA therapies like individualized neoantigen treatments.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's sci-fi story features a crashed spacecraft crew of three humans and an AI surviving on a hostile planet with alien dome structures. The article also discusses AI models considered too dangerous to release.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A stranded spaceship crew crash-lands on a hostile alien planet with limited resources. They discover ancient alien artifacts nearby and must explore to survive.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on consumer GPUs. The system improves graphics quality and performance, making advanced simulations accessible to everyday hardware.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling AI systems to process and compare text, images, and other data types together for improved search and ranking tasks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve while performing tasks in real-world environments, adapting their behaviors dynamically without retraining.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field. The conversation explores what innovations and challenges lie ahead for artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing workflows.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available for developers and farmers to improve farming practices through AI and digital solutions.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for the Gemini API: Flex and Priority, designed to balance cost and latency for different user needs.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation, editing, and sharing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technologies for high-quality video generation.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect the country's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most advanced open-source models designed for complex reasoning and agentic workflows, setting new capabilities in the open model landscape.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves audio AI with enhanced precision and lower latency for more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI applications.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI development.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, using generative AI to boost real-time photorealism in video games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions to generate enhanced details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and Cursor's trigger-based coding automations. ChatGPT and Anthropic also introduced interactive visualizations for math and science content.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing automated failure attribution.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI model performance.

articles.title: Liquid AI Releases LFM2.5-VL-450M: a 450M-Parameter Vision-Language Model with Bounding Box Prediction, Multilingual Support, and Sub-250ms Edge Inference
articles.summary: Liquid AI released LFM2.5-VL-450M, a 450M-parameter vision-language model with bounding box prediction, multilingual support, and function calling. It achieves sub-250ms edge inference on devices from embedded modules to flagship smartphones.

articles.title: Researchers from MIT, NVIDIA, and Zhejiang University Propose TriAttention: A KV Cache Compression Method That Matches Full Attention at 2.5× Higher Throughput
articles.summary: Researchers propose TriAttention, a KV cache compression method that matches full attention accuracy while achieving 2.5× higher throughput on long-chain reasoning tasks, reducing memory usage by 10.7×.

articles.title: How to Build a Secure Local-First Agent Runtime with OpenClaw Gateway, Skills, and Controlled Tool Execution
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a secure local-first OpenClaw agent runtime with authenticated gateway access, custom skills, and controlled tool execution for autonomous reasoning and orchestration.

articles.title: After using Lenovo's new Yoga laptop, I'm wondering if Windows makers are running out of ideas
articles.summary: Lenovo's Yoga 7a 2-in-1 offers versatility and solid build quality, but its mid-tier hardware struggles against competitors, raising questions about Windows laptop innovation.

articles.title: The $30 Google TV stick may be the budget Chromecast successor we've been waiting for
articles.summary: Walmart is releasing a $30 Google TV stick that could serve as the successor to Chromecast, offering budget-friendly streaming for fans seeking an affordable option.

articles.title: I stopped guessing which AA batteries are dead - this charging station keeps them in check for me
articles.summary: Olight's new Ostation 2 Pro battery charger eliminates guessing which AA batteries are dead by automatically monitoring and maintaining battery health.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article explores building a deterministic 3-tiered graph-RAG system that moves beyond traditional vector search, offering more structured and reliable retrieval methods for AI applications.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article discusses essential design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. Provides guidance on mastering approaches to creating autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: A practical guide to testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks for evaluation and quality assessment of agent performance.

articles.title: Advanced RAG Retrieval: Cross-Encoders & Reranking
articles.summary: This guide explores cross-encoders and reranking techniques to improve RAG retrieval pipelines. It emphasizes the importance of implementing a second pass in retrieval systems for better results.

articles.title: Why Every AI Coding Assistant Needs a Memory Layer
articles.summary: AI coding assistants require persistent memory layers to overcome LLM statelessness and improve code quality by maintaining context across sessions.

articles.title: Introduction to Reinforcement Learning Agents with the Unity Game Engine
articles.summary: An interactive guide to reinforcement learning agents using the Unity Game Engine, addressing one of machine learning's most complex areas.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights NVIDIA's advances in physical AI and robotics breakthroughs across industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game about grit and faith, is now available on GeForce NOW cloud streaming service alongside three other new games.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI deployment across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases improved foundation model versions. Understanding model lifecycle is crucial for planning AI applications and testing before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, available through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, helps enterprises manage agent sprawl by enabling discovery, sharing, and reuse of agents and tools across organizations while addressing visibility and governance challenges.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component enabling real-time visibility into AI agent browsing activities. Users can monitor agent interactions with websites directly in applications using just three lines of JSX code.

articles.title: MiniMax M2.7 Advances Scalable Agentic Workflows on NVIDIA Platforms for Complex AI Applications
articles.summary: MiniMax M2.7 enhances the M2.5 model with improvements for agentic workflows and complex applications in reasoning, ML research, software engineering, and office work on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm is an open-source job scheduler managing 65% of TOP500 systems. Organizations seek to integrate Slurm with Kubernetes for large-scale GPU workloads while preserving existing workflows.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables significant checkpoint cost reduction for LLM training using just 30 lines of Python. The tool compresses massive model snapshots, reducing storage costs for large-scale training operations.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media advertisements across various platforms, in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged diverse experiences to pioneer innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how non-traditional paths can lead to breakthrough developments in cutting-edge fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, advancing interpretability research to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects in noisy conditions.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm avoids temporal difference learning limitations, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks in off-policy reinforcement learning.
